steps.title: Identify the grammatical rule
steps.content: The sentence opens with two participial phrases: "Laden with emojis and seemingly meaningless words, and driven largely by Sawano’s ‘personal feeling and mood,’" A participial phrase at the start of a sentence must be followed immediately by the noun it modifies. The phrases describe the titles (not the listener, the pieces, or an approach), so "the titles" must be the subject of the main clause.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'the listener can approach each piece free from expectations because of the titles.': Places "the listener" right after the modifying phrases, implying the listener — not the titles — is laden with emojis and driven by Sawano’s mood. Dangling modifier. ✗
  • Choice B — 'the titles allow the listener to approach each piece free from expectations.': Places "the titles" immediately after the participial phrases, correctly establishing that the titles are what are laden with emojis and driven by Sawano’s mood. ✓
  • Choice C — 'each piece can be approached by the listener free from expectations because of the titles.': Places "each piece" right after the modifying phrases, implying the pieces — not the titles — are laden with emojis. Dangling modifier. ✗
  • Choice D — 'the listener’s approach to each piece because of the titles can be free from expectations.': Places "the listener’s approach" right after the phrases, implying the approach is laden with emojis and driven by Sawano’s mood. Dangling modifier. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
